/en/javascript/2020/11/11/pixijs-v5-tutorial-40-finale/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (40) - The Finale: A Year-Long Marathon Wraps Up
JavaScript
PixiJS V5 Tutorial
PixiJS
Canvas

This is the closing post for the whole PixiJS series, so let's wrap it up properly. I'll talk about why I started writing about Pixi in the first place, share the RPG battle system I ended up building at work, drop the full collection of example code, and thank the articles that made this whole series possible.
/en/javascript/2020/11/02/pixijs-v5-tutorial-39-sound/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (39) - Sound: Adding Music to Your Game with Pixi-Sound
PixiJS V5 教學

Music is one of those elements that makes a game feel alive. In this post we look at Pixi-Sound, PixiJS's official audio library, and wire some background music into the Treasure Hunter game we built earlier — including how to loop it so it doesn't just play once and stop.
/en/javascript/2020/10/31/pixijs-v5-38-texturepacker/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (38) - Packing Game Assets with TexturePacker

Most games cram a bunch of images into one big sheet and use a tool to generate a JSON file, which cuts down on load requests. This post walks through TexturePacker — installing it, importing your sprites, exporting a sprite sheet plus JSON for PixiJS, and even switching the UI to Chinese.
/en/javascript/2020/10/21/pixijs-v5-37-stats-js/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (37) - Adding stats.js to Watch Your FPS

Let's talk about stats.js. While we're at it, I'm switching to a different demo, because the treasure hunter we built earlier doesn't really stress the performance enough to see anything interesting. This post walks through adding stats.js, deliberately creating some lag, and what each of its panels actually means.
/en/javascript/2020/10/12/pixijs-v5-tutorial-36-fps/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (36) — Adding an FPS Counter to Your Game

We've finished a decent chunk of the Treasure Hunter game, so now it's a good time to talk about something PIXI keeps bringing up — FPS. In this post I'll explain what FPS actually is, why it matters for games, and how to drop a real FPS counter into our game using the stats.js library.
/en/javascript/2020/10/05/pixijs-v5-tutorial-35-polishing-details/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (35) - Polishing the Last Few Details

This is the last post in the series, so let's take our Treasure Hunter game and polish off a few rough edges — randomizing the monster speed on restart, making the player go semi-transparent on a hit, and dealing random collision damage instead of a fixed amount.
/en/javascript/2020/09/28/pixijs-v5-34-integrating-collision/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (34) - Wiring Up the Collision Function to Win the Game

Time to make the treasure chest follow the explorer around. Once you grab it and walk to the door, the game shows a win. The good news is this part is way simpler than the collision math we built earlier — it's mostly just calling our collision function in the right places and tracking one little flag.
/en/javascript/2020/09/21/pixijs-v5-33-making-monsters/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (33) - Spawning a Swarm of Monsters

Time to build the monsters. Right now we've only got one slime, which isn't much of a threat — so let's spawn a whole bunch of them and make them move on their own. We'll loop to generate the slimes, push them into an array, update them in the game loop, and give each one a random speed so they don't all march in sync.
/en/javascript/2020/09/14/pixijs-v5-32-restart-game-button/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (32) - Building a Restart Button From Scratch

A lighter one this time, picking up right where the last post left off. In this article we build a restart button for the Game Over screen — drawing the button with geometry, adding the label text, wiring up the click handler, and resetting everything back to its starting state.
/en/javascript/2020/09/07/pixijs-v5-31-text-messages/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (31): Showing Game Over and Start Text Messages

Time to build the text message that pops up when the player dies from a collision. The trick here isn't the text itself — it's juggling two Containers, one shown and one hidden. This post walks through the death screen, the start screen, and how to flip between them with PixiJS.
